body:
{
  "title": "Blake Nowak — Enterprise Account Executive, Anchor Codec",
  "compiled_truth": "Blake Nowak is an Enterprise Account Executive at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor), where he joined in September 2026 as the company's first dedicated enterprise sales hire. Before Anchor, Blake spent roughly seven years doing enterprise sales at two infrastructure companies — one focused on observability tooling and one on storage orchestration — and carried a reputation for consistent top-quartile quota attainment across both stints. He's the kind of seller who builds decks that read like short films and tells customer stories in a way that makes engineers lean forward.

Blake's arrival at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) coincided with the company's early push into enterprise pipeline development. At the time, Anchor's GTM motion was largely founder-led, and Nowak was brought on specifically to professionalize the outbound process and start building repeatable playbooks. He spent his first few weeks shadowing technical calls, absorbing the language around deterministic data infrastructure and single-pass primitives so he could translate it into business value for procurement teams and line-of-business buyers. Colleagues describe him as unusually good at bridging the gap between deeply technical founders and non-technical economic buyers — a skill that proved essential during Anchor's first wave of enterprise pilots in late 2026.

As a storyteller, Blake tends to lead with outcomes rather than architecture. His pitch narratives usually start with a customer pain point — data integrity failures, redundant processing pipelines, audit fatigue — and work backward to Anchor's primitives. He's been known to sketch entire customer journeys on a whiteboard in discovery calls, which sometimes annoys solutions engineers but almost always lands well with prospects. He also maintains a running internal doc he calls the "Win Tape," a detailed log of every closed deal including the narrative arc of how the deal moved through the funnel.

Nowak is based out of Chicago, though he travels frequently to Anchor's main office and to customer sites. He reports into the Head of GTM and works closely with the solutions engineering team. By mid-2027, Blake had closed several of Anchor's largest enterprise contracts and was informally mentoring two newer AEs hired after him. His quota attainment through Q2 2027 was reportedly above 130%, making him one of the highest-performing individual contributors at the company. He occassionally speaks on panels about infrastructure sales strategy, usually with a self-deprecating humor that belies how seriously he takes pipeline hygiene.",
  "timeline": "- **2026-09-15** | Blake Nowak joins [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) as the first dedicated Enterprise Account Executive.
- **2026-10-08** | Completes a two-week technical immersion with the engineering team; starts building the first outbound sales playbook for enterprise prospects.
- **2026-12-03** | Closes Anchor's first enterprise pilot deal — a 90-day proof-of-concept with a large financial services firm.
- **2027-02-14** | Delivers an internal "Win Tape" presentation covering lessons from the first three closed enterprise deals.
- **2027-04-22** | Participates in a panel on selling developer infrastructure at a GTM-focused conference in Austin.
- **2027-06-30** | Q2 2027 quota attainment reported at ~132%; recognized internally as top-performing AE.
- **2027-09-10** | Begins informally mentoring two new AEs joining the expanding [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) sales team.
- **2027-11-17** | Closes Anchor's largest enterprise contract to date — a multi-year agreement with a logistics platform.
- **2028-01-22** | Contributes to a revised enterprise pricing model alongside the GTM and finance teams.
- **2028-04-05** | Leads a customer storytelling workshop for the broader sales org, drawing from his "Win Tape" methodology."
}
